_comment: MILNET SSO — GCP Cloud Armor WAF Security Policy
_note: Apply via: gcloud compute security-policies import milnet-sso-waf --file-format=json --source=waf-rules.json
name: milnet-sso-waf
description: WAF rules for MILNET SSO system — SQL injection, XSS, size limits, rate limiting, geo-restrictions, bot detection
_test_scenarios: Each test below corresponds to a rule by priority number.
Test: Send GET /healthz from GCP LB probe IP 35.191.0.1
Test: Send GET /healthz from non-GCP IP 1.2.3.4
Expected: processed by subsequent rules (not explicitly allowed by this rule)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (DROP TABLE injection in POST body)
Test: curl 'https://sso/api/auth' -H 'X-Custom: 1 UNION SELECT password FROM users'
Expected: passes (benign query, no SQLi pattern)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (reflected XSS via script tag)
Expected: passes (benign text, no XSS pattern)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (absolute path to sensitive file)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (remote file inclusion via URL param)
Test: Send request with HTTP request smuggling payload (Content-Length + Transfer-Encoding)
Test: Send request patterns matching known scanner signatures
Test: curl -X POST with exactly 1048576 bytes
Priority 3000 — Global rate limit (100 req/min per IP)
Test: Send 100 requests from same IP within 60 seconds
Test: Send 101st request from same IP within 60 seconds
Test: Send 100 requests from IP-A and 100 from IP-B simultaneously
Expected: all 200 succeed (rate limit is per-IP, not global)
Test: Wait 60 seconds after hitting limit, then send 1 request
Priority 3100 — Auth endpoint rate limit (20 req/min per IP)
Test: Send 20 POST requests to /api/auth/login from same IP
Test: Send 21st POST to /api/auth/login from same IP
Test: Send 21st request to /api/users (non-auth endpoint) from same IP
Expected: 200 OK (non-auth endpoint uses the 100/min limit, not 20/min)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (direct internal path access blocked)
Expected: 403 Forbidden (attempt to reach TSS directly)
Expected: passes (legitimate request without internal headers)
Priority 5000 — Bot detection (known bad user agents)
Test: curl --no-keepalive 'https://sso/api/auth' (no User-Agent header at all)
Test: This rule is in preview mode by default (no enforcement)
Expected: all requests pass regardless of origin region
Test (when enabled): request from blocked region code
Test (when enabled): request from allowed region code
Test: curl 'https://sso/api/health' with valid UA, no attack patterns
Expected: 200 OK (passes all checks, allowed by catch-all rule)
Test: any request not matching priority 9000 allow rule
Test: send 10,000 requests/sec from distributed IPs
Expected: Cloud Armor adaptive protection triggers, begins blocking
Test: verify STANDARD ruleVisibility logs suspicious patterns
Expected: Cloud Armor security policy logs show anomaly detection events

rules.description: Allow health check probes from GCP load balancers
rules._test: GET /healthz from 35.191.0.1 -> 200; GET /healthz from 1.2.3.4 -> falls through to other rules
?id=1 OR 1=1 -> 403; username=admin'; DROP TABLE users;-- -> 403; ?name=alice -> pass
HTTP request smuggling (CL+TE) -> 403; HTTP response splitting -> 403
requests matching scanner fingerprints (probe paths, timing patterns) -> 403

rules.description: Request size limit — reject bodies > 1 MiB
rules._test: POST 2MB file -> 413; POST 512KB -> pass; POST 1048576 bytes -> pass; POST 1048577 bytes -> 413

rules.description: Rate limiting — 100 requests per minute per IP
rules._test: 100 reqs from same IP in 60s -> all pass; 101st -> 429; different IP -> independent counter; wait 60s -> counter resets

rules.description: Rate limiting — auth endpoints stricter (20 req/min per IP)
rules._test: 20 POST /api/auth/login from same IP -> pass; 21st -> 429; 21st to /api/users -> pass (different limit)

rules.description: Puzzle challenge bypass detection — block direct orchestrator probes
Bot detection — block known bad user agents
rules._test: UA 'sqlmap/1.7' -> 403; UA 'Nikto/2.1.6' -> 403; UA 'zgrab/0.x' -> 403; UA 'Shodan.io' -> 403; UA 'Mozilla/5.0 Chrome' -> pass

rules.description: Block requests with no User-Agent (likely automated)
rules._test: empty UA header -> 403; no UA header -> 403; UA 'my-client/1.0' -> pass

rules.description: Geographic restriction — allow only configured regions (default: all). Update srcIpRanges with geo-IP blocks to restrict.
rules._test: Preview mode: all requests pass. When enabled: region XX -> 403; allowed region -> pass
rules._note: Enable and configure region codes to restrict access by geography. Set preview=false to enforce.

rules.description: Allow legitimate traffic that passed all checks
rules._test: GET /api/health with valid UA, no attack patterns -> 200

adaptiveProtectionConfig._test: Send 10k req/sec from distributed IPs -> adaptive protection triggers and begins blocking; verify security policy logs show anomaly detection events
